disclosure_items.clinical_fact: 24 hours of watery diarrhoea and severe nausea followed by persistent vomiting, unable to keep any oral fluid down
trigger_question_examples: How long has this been going on?
Is she able to keep any fluids down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started about a day ago with watery diarrhoea and feeling really sick, and now she can't keep even a sip of water down — it comes straight back up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No urine passed in the last 12 hours despite the illness (reduced urine output)
trigger_question_examples: When did she last pass urine?
Has she been to the toilet to wee today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The staff say she hasn't passed any urine at all in the last twelve hours.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New confusion / altered mental state compared with baseline, reported by staff
trigger_question_examples: Is she her normal self, or does she seem confused?
Any change in how alert or clear-thinking she is?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She seems muddled and more confused than usual today — not quite with it the way she normally is.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe dizziness and weakness on sitting or standing; now bedbound although normally mobile with a walker
trigger_question_examples: What happens when she sits or stands up?
Is she able to stand or walk?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The room spins and she feels she'll faint whenever she's sat up, so she can't stand — she normally gets around with a walker but today she can't.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Her regular blood-pressure tablet (perindopril) has been withheld by nursing staff during the illness
trigger_question_examples: What regular medications does she take?
Have any of her usual medicines been stopped or held?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's on a blood-pressure tablet, perindopril, but the nurses have been holding it while she's been so unwell.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No severe localised abdominal pain and no vomiting of blood
trigger_question_examples: Does she have any severe tummy pain in one spot?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no bad pain in one particular spot and no blood in the vomit — it's mostly the being sick, the diarrhoea and the weakness.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: She's just so weak and sick — I don't think it's a sharp pain anywhere, no.
